Copy Blueprint - Week 6 - Session 12

Copy Blueprint - Week 6 - Session 2
Daniela – Weight loss
The Facebook testimonials pull quotes were used effectively with pictures – this give good social
proof to the reader / avatar. This is way better the Bob S from Wichita (some unscrupulous
people will fake these comments)
In CopyPro – In the benefit short and to the point with clear and to the point (goal for the USP is
15 – 30 words) this copy should be punchy.
There are 4 or 5 field groups in CopyPro that sound similar
· 	Benefits
· 	Offer
· 	Sales bullets
· 	Promises
All of these sound similar so they need to be modified so they don’t sound the same – the
wording has to be switched up so the copy isn’t duplicated.
Lose an average of 12 pounds in the first 4 weeks cant be said (This should not be repeated word
for word in a bullet, dream, promise or a USP)
Keep the fields short to make sure the inputs are to the point. (Amadeus movie quote – just take a
few notes out)
Bullets
Without – this is the turn or the close
Be sure to make bullets very specific – this keeps them interesting
Leave the periods out at the end of sentences. Let CopyPro do the hard work
11:30
Derek - Authorship
Make sure the numbers and content used is very specific – this helps in the niche to show you are
not an imposter.
Aspiring authors – good avatar
Keep the USP really short and to the point – no fluff is needed
With bullets the point is to be outlandish
Sleezy self promotion is something marketers and authors are scared
Reframe – It isn’t about __________ its about __________
It isn’t about being an author, its about being a best selling author
14:30
Bullets
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 7 --

The 2 most potent ways to annihilate writers block and they take less than 30 seconds each –
This is specific and isn’t “A potent way to write a best seller” – the original example is hyper
specific
Rid yourself of vs annihilate – the second is better
30 seconds each is intriguing
The turn doesn’t have to be with “without”
Ask questions in the bullets:
Should you give your book away for free? – This creates curiosity and know I want to know
Too much mystery can be a problem
The formula for bullets
Specificity and curiosity
Bullet example
2 quick tips to finally get consistent book sales on autopilot… without ever logging onto social
media or sending a single email
Quick tips + auto pilot makes this bullet = good bullet (specific + physical act)
If someone can see themselves in the bullet it has a better chance of working
18:45 – Lesson for the day
Bullet tip #1
If the bullet sounds exactly like a benefit statement it is wrong. I bullet should be hyper specific
and preferably intriguing (Look at the examples built into CopyPro)
$50 million blueprint
· 	You’ll find a deceptively simple way to lose 5 pounds or more the first week
Deceptively simple = curiosity
· 	You’ll discover how so-called experts who have never been overweight a day in ther
lives influences you negatively and leads you to stubborn fat loss mode
· 	Warning don’t even think of trying to set a weight loss or body shaping goal and stick
with it until you learn how trying “done for you solutions” like Jenny Craig or Weight Watchers
can sabotage your results.
· 	How to quickly and easily burn fat, keep your thyroid hormones optimized, look younger,
and feel better than you have in decades without counting calories, starving yourself, exercising
daily or doing without your favorite foods. (this bullet is way to long – this is also no different
as a benefit)
· A “Sneaky” (I cant believe this works!) way to reboot your lagging thyroid gland so you lose
weight at night… is it good for you? The surprising answer on page __________ (this is a kick
ass bullet)
· 	You’ll get a surefire remedy for love handles – your going to love this (ultra specific)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 7 --

22:00
Bullet tip #2
To get specific and build curiosity, think “timeline” , “numbers” and “questions” (look at the
examples in CopyPro)
The three things to focus on if I get stuck in writing bullets
· 	Timeline
· 	Numbers
· 	Questions
__________ (#) Ways to
__________ (#) ways to __________ (action) in _________ (time) or less
Do you know the (question) __________ (#) ways to __________ (action) in _________ (time)
or less
23:20
Jon takes a stand alone bullet and turns it into a question
Are there really (question) 3 clever ways to burn fat in 7 days… works even if your thighs are
the size of sequoia tree trucks
A “Sneaky” (I cant believe this works!) way to reboot your lagging thyroid gland so you lose
weight at night… is it good for you? (question) The surprising answer on page __________
Not in the class – types of questions that could be added
· 	Is it good for you
· 	Are you making this mistake
· 	Could you be next
· 	Whats the worst that could happen
· 	Are you really ready
· 	Can this cost you big time
· 	What will you do when this happens
· 	Is this the best time
· 	Are all of the so called “Gurus” wrong
Want a surefire way to lose your love handles… Just don’t do this one thing and you can
25:00
Bullet tip #3
A mysterious secret from ancient Egypt for weight loss involving a pebble in your left front
pocket. (this was borrowed from Sir Gary Halbert – this came from the astrology niche)
Is there a way that this bullet seen on the screen can be seen as a benefit.
This statement gets people to say “What the hell is that” – this is the point of a bullet or
fascination as Mel Martin called them. Get the people to ask a question.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 7 --

· 	How can I get that
· 	How can I do that
· 	What is that and how does it work
· 	What did that just say
· 	Can you tell me more
This is the point of a fascination
A benefit or promise is a straight fact or idea that someone doesn’t really need help reading into.
Killer bullets get people to want to ask questions or find out more.
A secret from ancient Egypt for weight loss involving a pebble in your left front pocket….
Curious turn to page 7
The pebble is meant to be an anchor to the goal – the left front pocket is specific
27:00
Back to CopyPro
$50 million letter – weight loss
Success traits are not in the offer – its means to be that way
Success myths are talked about –
After a long journey filled with trial and errors – is there a common trait – success traits
Offer set up – I looked at all of these people who had this one thing in common
This left me with one powerful truth… if you want to succeed you will want to __________
I spent the last 14 years analyzing … (USP)
The USP was broken into 3 paragraphs
Introducing the 4 day diet
Role models
What is behind the success
Rarity –
One thing fixes everything (one thing they all have in common)
I worked with people over time and this is what I came up with
Worlds foremost …
All letters in $50 million template can be used
You can…
You can…
You can…
Bullets – The bullet formula doesn’t have to be used when you know the formulas
Jon sees things all of the time after the letter CopyPro produces – he changes them sometimes.
Perfection is the enemy of progress.
32:30 – Questions and answers

-- 4 of 7 --

There isn’t homework over the weekend – just work on my project
Students mention the process is getting easier
Dovi – Are headlines covered in the course? – There is some – CopyPro writes tons of headlines
but we all should know and understand the structure (week 8 – q and a / dissecting copy)
35:00
How many bullets is too many – There is no real answer … keep going as long as they are
interesting
Valerie - Most of Jons bullets start with You’ll, should this always be done to make them
parallel – This was done in this copy for a reason – mix and match in the copy
Joel – Can there be too much value in the bonuses hurt the offer? – no – This can be a problem if
they are not complementary. They should not be competitive.
The 4 day diet blueprint + the cook book + recipe guide = sounds like more stuff – a diet plan is
not a cookbook
What inside my product can I pull out and make 2 parts or 3 parts? Jon pulled subject lines out of
the original emailpro and sold it as an add on or a bonus. The sales page templates could be used
as well.
Tip – Come up with bonuses inside the product to separate them out later .. also comes with..
also comes with..
Dovi – Can you start bullets with different tenses? – Yes and you should, there should be
multiple variations. Some of the bullets used in Jons copy were actually benefits but they were
indented.
VSL – example
Jon has been working on a VSL and found that the copy was too repetitive. When repetitive copy
is used the reader gets fatigue and loses interest in the copy. If the reader can predict what is
going to be said it causes people to drop out.
Structurally bad copy is going to be a problem – there isn’t a way to point out every winner or
loser with pieces of copy. The whole idea is to test. Look for places where copy may have an
issue for the lack of closing buyers.
Some brilliant copy doesn’t work and some poorly written copy does – it comes down to testing.
Harlan Kilstein – He put copy from John Carlton in COC and framed it as “This copy is from
new copywriters” and saw reactions across the board. Some people saw the copy and knew who
wrote it some people gave their .02 and said it sucked. (that was an interesting thread…)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 7 --

Copywriters judge copy on if they like it not if it works.. this is a mistake. “You are hiring me to
write this because you suck at this, you are great at other things… go do than and let me do it the
right way”. – Shock value and showing proof of being an expert.
When looking at other peoples copy the real thing to do is test to look at it if it is structurally
correct.
Nothing works all of the time ever except mathematical laws.
47:50
Do bullets need to be an a particular order – Jon likes to mix it up and does it by feel. Its not
about strong or weak. With benefits Jon likes to lead off with strong and end strong. (Big finish –
big ending)
Bullets are like throwing spaghetti at the wall, but they all should be written will with intrigue
Judy – Is it a case of best guesses when it comes to copy?
Philosophy – How do we know something is true?
There is the truth and there are subjective rules – One of the best VSLs jon though was terrible
because he felt it lacked. When it was read from Porter Stansbury read the letter it sounded auful.
The End of America promo killed it with $100,000,000 in sales
51:00
It is important to look for my own biases in copy. This could cloud my judgement for what
works and what does not (the rule is to be structurally sound and test) I will tend to champion my
copy. It is important to Copy Cub the work I do.
Sir Gary Halbert “Test it, let the audience decide
Pre frames are important when it comes to writing
There are certain formulas that are effective most of the time in copy
52:30
Lianna – What is the best order to write copy in?
Headlines, bullets or copy first? – Jon Carlton likes to start with bullets, Jon likes to start with
headlines.
Headlines can be pulled from CopyPro to get a start or to use for copy
Jon says the headline can set the tone for the entire letter, this is one of the reasons he starts here.
Jon Benson writes from top down – headline and then copy
John Carlton writes from the middle out – Bullets and offer
There isn’t a perfect way to do this
“Great copy is assembled” – Eugene Schwartz
Writing bullets first does help with creativity. This helps juice up the copy. What is written in the
copy needs to be fulfilled in the product even if it hasn’t been invented yet.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
